protocol.protocol_name: CXCR4-EV miR-29b Delivery to Primary HSCs
protocol.objective: Validate that CXCR4-targeted EVs loaded with miR-29b reduce Smad2/3 nuclear translocation and collagen I expression in primary mouse hepatic stellate cells.

steps.title: HSC Isolation
steps.procedure: Perfuse mouse liver, mince tissue, digest with collagenase, culture on uncoated dish, collect cells after 24 h.

steps.title: EV Treatment
steps.procedure: Plate 5x10^4 HSCs/well in 12-well plates, add 10 µg EVs (CXCR4-targeted or control) in serum-free medium.

steps.title: Smad2/3 Nuclear Translocation Assay
steps.procedure: Fix cells with 4% PFA, permeabilize, stain with anti-Smad2/3 and DAPI, image by fluorescence microscopy.

steps.title: Collagen I mRNA Quantification
steps.procedure: Extract RNA, reverse-transcribe, run qPCR with collagen I primers, normalize to GAPDH.

steps.title: EV Characterization
steps.procedure: Measure size by NTA (~100-150 nm) and verify peptide by Western blot.

controls.negative: Non-targeted EVs loaded with scrambled miR

protocol.if_positive_then: Advance to in vivo CCl4-induced fibrosis model to test therapeutic efficacy.
protocol.if_negative_then: Re-optimize EV loading or peptide targeting; consider alternative miRNAs.

datasets.summary: Three Zenodo datasets provide data on liver fibrosis models, HSC activation, and Smad pathway modulation. The iPSC-derived liver spheroid dataset can be mined for miR‑29b effects on Smad2/3 targets and collagen I expression. The early fibrosis tissue dataset offers spatial data to correlate CXCR4 expression in HSCs with Smad nuclear translocation and collagen deposition. The Prominin‑1/SMAD7 dataset demonstrates that Smad inhibition reduces collagen I, supporting the hypothesis that CXCR4‑targeted EVs delivering miR‑29b would similarly suppress Smad signaling and fibrosis.

datasets.title: Human iPSC-derived liver co-culture spheroids to model liver fibrosis
datasets.why_relevant: Contains human iPSC-derived hepatic stellate cell (HSC) spheroids with transcriptomic and possibly small RNA data, allowing assessment of miR-29b impact on Smad2/3 target genes and collagen I (COL1A1) expression.
datasets.analysis: Extract gene expression matrix for HSC compartment; perform differential expression between miR-29b mimic or EV treatment groups and controls; quantify changes in COL1A1 and Smad2/3 pathway genes (SMAD2, SMAD3, TGFB1, COL1A1, ACTA2); if immunofluorescence images are available, quantify nuclear Smad2/3 signal using image analysis pipelines.
datasets.expected_signal: >=30% reduction in COL1A1 mRNA and >=40% decrease in nuclear Smad2/3 intensity in miR-29b-treated spheroids compared to controls.

datasets.title: STRUCTURAL AND CELLULAR CHANGES IN LIVER TISSUE DURING CHRONIC HEPATITIS AND EARLY FIBROSIS FORMATION
datasets.why_relevant: Provides histological and spatial transcriptomic data from mouse liver with early fibrosis, including HSC activation markers, collagen deposition, and CXCR4 expression, enabling correlation of CXCR4 levels with Smad2/3 nuclear translocation and collagen I expression.
datasets.analysis: If spatial transcriptomics or single-cell RNA-seq is present, isolate HSC clusters; quantify CXCR4, SMAD2/3 nuclear localization (via IHC scores), and COL1A1 expression; compute Pearson correlation between CXCR4 expression and Smad2/3 nuclear score; compare CXCR4-high vs CXCR4-low HSC groups for collagen I levels.
datasets.expected_signal: Positive correlation of high CXCR4 with reduced nuclear Smad2/3 and lower COL1A1 expression, supporting CXCR4-mediated Smad inhibition.

datasets.title: Hepatocyte-specific Prominin-1 protects against liver injury-induced fibrosis by stabilizing SMAD7
datasets.why_relevant: Focuses on SMAD7, an inhibitory Smad that blocks Smad2/3 signaling, and shows its effect on collagen I expression in a fibrosis model, providing a parallel mechanism to miR-29b-mediated Smad inhibition.
datasets.analysis: Perform differential expression between Prominin-1 overexpressing and control groups; assess SMAD7 levels, COL1A1 expression, and Smad2/3 target genes; calculate pathway enrichment for TGF‑β/Smad signaling; quantify nuclear Smad2/3 via any available imaging data.
datasets.expected_signal: Upregulation of SMAD7 associated with >=30% decrease in COL1A1 and >=40% reduction in nuclear Smad2/3, indicating that Smad inhibition can attenuate collagen production.

protocol.protocol_name: Dual-Ligand EV miR-29b Delivery to Activated HSCs
protocol.objective: Test whether CXCR4/alpha v beta 3-decorated EVs loaded with miR-29b reduce Smad2/3 nuclear translocation and collagen I mRNA in mouse activated HSCs.

materials.item: Integrin alpha v beta 3 ligand (RGD peptide)

steps.title: Activate HSCs
steps.procedure: Plate HSCs on collagen I-coated plates, treat with 5 ng/mL TGF-beta1 for 24 h.

steps.title: Engineer EVs
steps.procedure: Mix EVs with CXCR4-binding T140 and RGD peptides (1 ug each per ug EV) for 30 min, then electroporate miR-29b mimic (10 pmol per ug EV).
steps.critical_parameter: Peptide/EV and miR/EV ratios to retain membrane integrity

steps.title: Label EVs
steps.procedure: Add PKH26 dye to engineered EVs (1:1000) and incubate 5 min at RT.

steps.title: Treat HSCs
steps.procedure: Add engineered EVs at 10 ug/mL to HSC cultures; include naked EV and miR-29b alone controls.

steps.title: Assess Smad nuclear translocation
steps.procedure: Fix cells, immunostain for Smad2/3 and DAPI, quantify nuclear Smad2/3 intensity by confocal microscopy.
steps.critical_parameter: Threshold for nuclear vs cytoplasmic Smad signal

steps.title: Measure collagen I expression
steps.procedure: Extract RNA, run qRT-PCR for Col1a1; normalize to GAPDH.

protocol.if_positive_then: Scale up to pilot in vivo CCl4-fibrosis model with systemic EV infusion.
protocol.if_negative_then: Re-optimize EV surface density or miR-29b loading; test alternative ligands.

datasets.summary: The selected datasets provide transcriptomic and spatial data from human iPSC‑derived liver spheroids and mouse liver tissue undergoing early fibrosis. They can be mined to confirm that activated hepatic stellate cells (HSCs) express CXCR4 and integrin αvβ3, that miR‑29b is suppressed, and that Smad2/3 signaling and collagen I are up‑regulated. This supports the mechanistic premise of the hypothesis that CXCR4/αvβ3‑targeted EVs delivering miR‑29b could block Smad signaling and reduce fibrosis. However, none of the datasets contain engineered EV treatment data, so they cannot directly test the efficacy of the described EVs.

datasets.title: Human iPSC-derived liver co-culture spheroids to model liver fibrosis
datasets.why_relevant: Contains RNA‑seq data from co‑culture spheroids that include hepatic stellate cells under fibrotic stimulation, allowing assessment of CXCR4, integrin αvβ3 (ITGAV/ITGB3), miR‑29b, SMAD2/3 and COL1A1 expression in activated versus quiescent HSCs.
datasets.analysis: 1. Identify HSC‑specific samples (e.g., by cell‑type markers such as DESMIN, GFAP). 2. Extract raw counts for CXCR4, ITGAV, ITGB3, miR‑29b, SMAD2, SMAD3, COL1A1. 3. Perform differential expression (DESeq2) between activated (TGF‑β or CCl4‑treated) and control spheroids. 4. Quantify fold‑change and adjust p‑values. 5. Correlational analysis between miR‑29b levels and SMAD2/3 target genes (e.g., COL1A1, ACTA2). 6. Visualize expression heatmaps and violin plots.
datasets.expected_signal: CXCR4 and ITGB3 up‑regulated in activated HSCs; miR‑29b down‑regulated; SMAD2/3 target genes (COL1A1, ACTA2) up‑regulated; positive correlation between low miR‑29b and high SMAD activity.

datasets.title: STRUCTURAL AND CELLULAR CHANGES IN LIVER TISSUE DURING CHRONIC HEPATITITIS AND EARLY FIBROSIS FORMATION
datasets.why_relevant: Provides histological and spatial transcriptomics data from mouse liver at early fibrosis stages, enabling quantification of CXCR4 and αvβ3 integrin expression, miR‑29b levels, SMAD2/3 nuclear localization, and collagen I deposition in regions enriched for activated HSCs.
datasets.analysis: 1. Locate regions of interest (ROIs) with high α‑SMA staining indicative of activated HSCs. 2. Use image analysis (e.g., QuPath) to measure fluorescence intensity for CXCR4 and integrin αvβ3 antibodies. 3. Perform in‑situ hybridization quantification for miR‑29b within the same ROIs. 4. Assess SMAD2/3 nuclear translocation by co‑localization of SMAD2/3 staining with DAPI. 5. Quantify collagen I deposition via Masson’s trichrome or collagen I immunofluorescence. 6. Compare intensities between early fibrotic and normal tissue; calculate percent reduction in Smad nuclear signal and collagen I area.
datasets.expected_signal: Elevated CXCR4 and αvβ3 integrin staining in activated HSC zones; reduced miR‑29b signal; increased SMAD2/3 nuclear intensity; increased collagen I area. This pattern predicts that restoring miR‑29b via CXCR4/αvβ3‑targeted EVs would diminish SMAD nuclear translocation and collagen deposition.

protocol.objective: Test whether CXCR4-targeted, FAP-displaying EVs loaded with miR-29b reduce Smad2/3 nuclear translocation and hepatic collagen I in CCl4-induced mouse liver fibrosis.

materials.item: Targeting peptide cocktail (CXCR4, SP94, FAP, 30 mg total)

steps.title: CCl4 induction
steps.procedure: Inject CCl4 i.p. 0.5 mg/kg thrice weekly for 2 weeks to induce fibrosis.

steps.title: Engineer EVs
steps.procedure: Conjugate targeting peptide cocktail to EVs via click chemistry, then load miR-29b by electroporation (250 V, 1000 µF).

steps.title: EV dosing
steps.procedure: Administer engineered EVs i.v. (1×10^10 particles per mouse) daily for 7 days.

steps.title: Tissue harvest & HSC isolation
steps.procedure: Euthanize mice, isolate primary HSCs using pronase/collagenase digestion, and collect liver samples for RNA/protein.

steps.title: Readouts
steps.procedure: Assess Smad2/3 nuclear translocation by immunofluorescence on HSCs; quantify collagen I mRNA by qPCR and protein by Western blot from liver extracts.

protocol.if_positive_then: Advance to chronic fibrosis model and evaluate dose-response and off-target biodistribution.
protocol.if_negative_then: Optimize peptide conjugation and miR-29b loading; repeat HSC assay.

datasets.summary: The available datasets contain liver fibrosis models and SMAD pathway data, allowing assessment of miR‑29b’s effect on collagen I expression and SMAD activity, but none provide data on CXCR4‑targeted extracellular vesicles or HSC‑specific surface markers. Consequently, the hypothesis cannot be fully validated without additional experimental data.

datasets.title: Human iPSC-derived liver co-culture spheroids to model liver fibrosis
datasets.why_relevant: Provides an in vitro liver fibrosis model with transcriptomic (including small RNA‑seq) data that can be interrogated for miR‑29b expression, collagen I (COL1A1) mRNA levels, and SMAD2/3 target gene activity.
datasets.analysis: Extract small RNA‑seq counts for miR‑29b and bulk RNA‑seq counts for COL1A1, COL3A1, ACTA2, CTGF, and SMAD2/3 target genes. Perform differential expression analysis comparing miR‑29b mimic‑treated spheroids to untreated controls. If miR‑29b data are not present, correlate miR‑29b expression (when available) with collagen I and SMAD‑target gene expression across all conditions.
datasets.expected_signal: Reduced COL1A1 and SMAD2/3 downstream gene expression in samples with elevated miR‑29b, supporting Smad pathway inhibition by miR‑29b.

datasets.title: STRUCTURAL AND CELLULAR CHANGES IN LIVER TISSUE DURING CHRONIC HEPATITIS AND EARLY FIBROSIS FORMATION
datasets.why_relevant: Contains liver tissue transcriptomic and spatial data from early fibrosis stages, enabling assessment of COL1A1 expression, SMAD2/3 nuclear translocation markers, and miR‑29b levels across disease progression.
datasets.analysis: Extract bulk RNA‑seq or spatial transcriptomics counts for COL1A1, SMAD2, SMAD3, and miR‑29b from normal, chronic hepatitis, and early fibrosis samples. Quantify SMAD2/3 nuclear localization using available immunofluorescence metadata if present. Compute Pearson correlation between miR‑29b expression and collagen I mRNA; test for a negative correlation. Compare SMAD2/3 activity markers between high‑ and low‑miR‑29b groups.
datasets.expected_signal: Higher COL1A1 and SMAD2/3 activity in early fibrosis with concomitant lower miR‑29b levels; a negative correlation would support the hypothesis that miR‑29b suppresses collagen I via SMAD inhibition.

datasets.title: Hepatocyte-specific Prominin-1 protects against liver injury-induced fibrosis by stabilizing SMAD7
datasets.why_relevant: Provides experimental data on SMAD pathway modulation (SMAD7) and its impact on collagen I expression in a mouse fibrosis model, offering indirect evidence for the efficacy of SMAD inhibition in reducing fibrosis.
datasets.analysis: Extract gene expression data for COL1A1, SMAD2, SMAD3, and SMAD7 from control and Prominin‑1 overexpression mice. Perform differential expression analysis to compare fibrotic versus SMAD7‑enhanced conditions. Evaluate whether increased SMAD7 correlates with decreased collagen I and SMAD2/3 target gene expression.
datasets.expected_signal: Elevated SMAD7 associated with reduced COL1A1 and SMAD2/3 downstream genes, supporting the concept that SMAD pathway inhibition leads to fibrosis attenuation.
